summary: Peruvian Nobel laureate novelist and one of the central figures of the Latin American Boom. His most frequently clued works are Aunt Julia and the Scriptwriter (whose even chapters consist of increasingly deranged radio serials by Pedro Camacho), The Time of the Hero (set at the Leoncio Prado Military Academy), The Feast of the Goat (about Dominican dictator Rafael Trujillo), The Green House (about a brothel in the desert near Piura), Conversation in the Cathedral, and The War of the End of the World. Ran for the presidency of Peru in 1990. Also known for his literary feud with Gabriel Garcia Marquez and his critical study of Flaubert, The Perpetual Orgy.

works.name: Aunt Julia and the Scriptwriter
works.description: Vargas Llosa's most frequently clued novel. A semi-autobiographical story about law student Mario's romance with an older divorcee, Aunt Julia. The even-numbered chapters consist of radio soap operas (radionovelas) written by the Bolivian scriptwriter Pedro Camacho for Radio Panamericana, which grow increasingly surreal and jumbled as Camacho descends into madness. Wikipedia

works.name: The Time of the Hero
works.description: Vargas Llosa's first novel (1963), originally titled La Ciudad y los Perros (The City and the Dogs). Set at the Leoncio Prado Military Academy in Lima, it follows cadets including the Jaguar, the Slave, and the Poet (Alberto). The Jaguar leads a gang called 'The Circle' that steals exam answers. The Slave is killed, and the Poet (Alberto) is exposed for selling pornographic stories. The novel was so controversial that copies were burned at the military academy. Wikipedia

works.name: The Feast of the Goat
works.description: Novel about the Dominican dictator Rafael Trujillo. Weaves together three narrative threads: Trujillo brooding over his impotence on the night of his assassination, his assassins plotting to kill him, and Urania Cabral returning decades later to confront her paralyzed father Agustin ('Egghead'), who let Trujillo rape her for political advancement. Characters include Lieutenant Amadito and the sinister Colonel Johnny Abbes. Wikipedia

works.name: The Green House
works.description: Novel (1966) set near the town of Piura in the deserts of northern Peru. The title refers to a brothel built by the wandering blind harp player Don Anselmo, later rebuilt by his daughter Chunga. Characters include the prostitute nicknamed 'Wildflower' (Bonifacia), the foundling whose eyes and tongue were eaten by vultures, and the giant Bolas. Lituma also appears. Wikipedia

works.name: Conversation in the Cathedral
works.description: Novel in which journalist Santiago Zavala meets his father's old chauffeur Ambrosio at a dog pound and they talk in the title bar. Opens with the famous line 'At what precise moment had Peru fucked itself up?' Santiago discovers his father Fermin (known as 'Gold Ball') had secret gay affairs. A dachshund is beaten to death in an early scene. Satirizes Peruvian politics. Wikipedia

works.name: The War of the End of the World
works.description: Novel based on the Canudos rebellion in Brazil, inspired by Euclides da Cunha's Os Sertoes (Rebellion in the Backlands). Features the Counselor who gains religious followers, the near-sighted journalist (likely based on da Cunha), and the red-headed, phrenology-loving Scotsman Galileo Gall who joins the rebellion. Wikipedia

works.name: The Bad Girl
works.description: Novel that reimagines Madame Bovary. The title character, finally revealed to be named Otilia, adopts various guises including Cuban revolutionary Comrade Arlette, Yakuza mistress Kuriko, and diplomat's wife Mme. Arnoux. She is loved throughout by Ricardo. She endures abuse from the Japanese smuggler Fukuda and recovers at a sanatorium. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
Mario Vargas Llosa is a Peruvian Nobel Prize-winning novelist and one of the central figures of the Latin American literary Boom. His most frequently asked-about work in quizbowl is Aunt Julia and the Scriptwriter, a semi-autobiographical novel in which the young law student Mario falls in love with and marries an older divorced relative, Aunt Julia. The novel's distinctive structure alternates between Mario's narration and the increasingly unhinged radio soap operas (radionovelas) written by the Bolivian scriptwriter Pedro Camacho for Radio Panamericana. Camacho's serials feature recurring absurdities: characters described as being in the 'prime of life' at fifty, the repeated name Ezequiel Delfin due to his poor memory, anti-Argentine bigotry, a Jehovah's Witness who threatens self-castration, and eventually all characters dying in a fire as Camacho descends into madness and is committed to an asylum.

His first novel, The Time of the Hero (originally La Ciudad y los Perros / The City and the Dogs), is set at the Leoncio Prado Military Academy and follows cadets including the Jaguar (who leads 'The Circle' gang and kills the Slave), the Slave, and the Poet (Alberto, who writes pornography for classmates). Faulkner inspired its nonlinear structure.

The Feast of the Goat portrays Dominican dictator Rafael Trujillo's regime through three intertwined narratives: Trujillo brooding over his impotence on the night of his assassination, four assassins plotting the killing, and Urania Cabral returning decades later to confront her paralyzed father Agustin ('Egghead'), who allowed Trujillo to rape her for political gain. Characters include Lieutenant Amadito and Colonel Johnny Abbes.

The Green House is set near Piura in northern Peru and centers on a brothel built in the desert by the blind harp player Don Anselmo, later rebuilt by his daughter Chunga. Key characters include the prostitute 'Wildflower' (Bonifacia), a foundling whose eyes and tongue were eaten by vultures, and the giant Bolas.

Conversation in the Cathedral opens with the iconic line 'At what precise moment had Peru fucked itself up?' and follows journalist Santiago Zavala meeting his father's chauffeur Ambrosio at a dog pound and talking in the title bar. Santiago discovers his father Fermin's secret gay affairs (known as 'Gold Ball').

The War of the End of the World fictionalizes the Canudos rebellion in Brazil, inspired by Euclides da Cunha's Os Sertoes. It features a near-sighted journalist (based on da Cunha), the religious leader the Counselor, and the red-headed Scottish phrenologist Galileo Gall.

The Bad Girl reimagines Madame Bovary through a title character (real name Otilia) who adopts many identities — Comrade Arlette, Kuriko, Mme. Arnoux — while always loved by Ricardo.

Biographically, Vargas Llosa ran for the Peruvian presidency in 1990, had a famous feud with Gabriel Garcia Marquez (punching him at a 1976 movie premiere in Mexico City, shouting 'That's for what you did to Patricia'), and was represented by the literary agent Carmen Balcells. His ex-wife published a memoir titled 'What Little [Vargas Llosa] Didn't Say.' His critical works include The Perpetual Orgy (on Flaubert), 'The Temptation of the Impossible' (on Victor Hugo), and Letters to a Young Novelist.

cards.front: Author: this author's feud with Garcia Marquez began in Barcelona, where Garcia Marquez privately consoled this author's wife
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: this author denounced Garcia Marquez as 'Castro's cortesano'
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: this author ran for the presidency of Peru in 1990
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: this author's Nobel acceptance speech condemns the 'bloody witches' Sabbath of the Chinese Cultural Revolution'
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: this author's ex-wife wrote a memoir titled 'What Little [this author] Didn't Say' claiming he fell in love with her niece
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: in Sabers and Utopias, this author recounts telling Jose Donoso on his deathbed 'Henry James is shit'
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Author: this author's 1971 doctoral thesis analyzes Garcia Marquez's novels by comparing writing to 'Deicide'
cards.back: Mario Vargas Llosa

cards.front: Work: a double biography of Paul Gauguin and his grandmother Flora Tristan
cards.back: The Way to Paradise (Mario Vargas Llosa)

cards.front: Work: book-length study of Madame Bovary in which this author hailed Flaubert as the first modern novelist
cards.back: The Perpetual Orgy (Mario Vargas Llosa)

cards.front: Work: this novel features Joseph Conrad saying 'You've deflowered me...about the Congo Free State' and the 'Black Diaries' of Roger Casement
cards.back: The Dream of the Celt (Mario Vargas Llosa)

cards.front: Work: protagonist Pantaleon Pantoja cheats on wife Pochita and supplies prostitutes to army barracks in this novel
cards.back: Captain Pantoja and the Special Service (Mario Vargas Llosa)

cards.front: Work: an insurance agent orders his wife Dona Lucrecia into erotic scenarios inspired by paintings by Boucher and Titian
cards.back: The Notebooks of Don Rigoberto (Mario Vargas Llosa)
